channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South Korea's worst wildfires are now almost contained following rain and cooler weather
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The most destructive wildfires ever to hit South Korea were almost contained, authorities announced Friday, after rain and cooler temperatures helped fire crew put out the blazes that have killed 28 people and razed vast swaths of land since last week.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration moves to end union rights for many federal workers
item.description: The order targets agencies it says have a national security mission but many of the departments don’t have a strict national security connection.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, causing high-rise collapse
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, killing at least two people in Bangkok and burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The US is hunting for eggs in Europe
item.description: It is common in parts of Europe for consumers to buy eggs that still have feathers and chicken poop stuck to them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NFL legend Randy White fights fentanyl after personal tragedy
item.description: Jesse Dempsey, a professional snowboarder, unknowingly took a pill laced with fentanyl, believing it was a prescribed painkiller for a knee injury.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dorman, NewsNation

item.title: Trial of former President Sarkozy sheds light on France’s back-channel talks with Libya’s Gadhafi
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The monthslong trial of former French President Nicolas Sarkozy over the alleged illegal financing of his 2007 presidential campaign is shedding light on France's back-channel talks with the government of then-Libyan leader Moammar Gadhafi.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under U.N.-sponsored external governance, boasts battlefield gains
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin proposed Friday to put Ukraine under external governance under the U.N. aegis as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement, a blustery statement that reflected the Kremlin leader's determination to achieve his war goals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Vance and wife to tour US military base in Greenland after diplomatic spat over uninvited visit
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance and his wife are due to visit an American military base in Greenland on Friday in a trip that was scaled back after an uproar among Greenlanders and Danes who were irked that the original itinerary was planned without consulting them.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP CROWTHER and KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Idaho murders suspect Bryan Kohberger penned essay showing he knew how to cover tracks
item.description: Bryan Kohberger wrote an essay on how to handle a crime scene investigation while studying at DeSales University, two years before the Idaho student murders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rich parents pay top dollar for high school spring break trips as vacation tragedies rattle families
item.description: Parents looking to send their kids on safe spring break trips are shelling out thousands of dollars for chaperoned travel accommodations as horror stories abroad increase.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chicago dubbed America's murder capital as Democrat leaders 'demonize' police, splurge on migrants: alderman
item.description: Chicago's 41st Ward Alderman Anthony Napolitano slammed the sanctuary city's administration as homicides continue to plague neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colleges rely on federal research funding. Under Trump, its future is in doubt
item.description: After decades of partnership with the U.S. government, colleges are facing new doubts about the future of their federal funding.
item.dc:creator: SHARON LURYE and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the Houthi rebels early Friday, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about Boulder, Colorado, the Sundance Film Festival's new home
item.description: The Sundance Film Festival is Boulder bound, leaving its home of four decades in Park City, Utah, for a new chapter in neighboring Colorado. Organizers announced their decision Thursday after a yearlong search in which numerous U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil's lawyers to appear in New Jersey court over jurisdiction of Columbia activist's case
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — Lawyers for Mahmoud Khalil, a Columbia University student the Trump administration is trying to expel from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Massachusetts mayor who shuttered a libelous newspaper faces renewed corruption allegations
item.description: EVERETT, Mass. (AP) — For years, the mayor of a Boston suburb dreaded Wednesdays. That was the day when a local weekly would publish shocking allegations that he was on the take, sexually harassing women or under investigation by the FBI.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stanford, Cal and UCLA investigated in Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: The Trump administration has opened investigations into the admissions policies at Stanford University and three campuses within the University of California system, including UC Berkeley, UCLA and UC Irvine, the Department of Justice said Thursday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who express their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Americans’ outlook on economy becomes a bit more pessimistic: Poll
item.description: Around 57 percent of those earning under $50,000 said their financial situation is “bad,” while 39 percent argued it is “good,” the poll found.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KREX 5

item.title: Poll of scientists says large majority weighing leaving US
item.description: The scientific journal Nature poll found that 75.3 percent of scientists are considering leaving the U.S. after the administration cut funding for research.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KTLA 5

item.title: Suspect in stolen van arrested after high-speed chase in Santa Monica
item.description: A suspect was arrested after leading police on a high-speed chase with a stolen van in Santa Monica Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Another federal judge blocks Trump policy banning transgender troops in the military
item.description: A U.S. judge in Washington state has blocked enforcement of President Donald Trump’s order banning transgender people from serving in the military.
item.dc:creator: Gene Johnson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge says heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional as states face mounting lawsuits
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A federal judge's rebuke of Texas housing prisoners in lockups without air conditioning stopped short of ordering a fix before summer in what has become of the country's biggest lawsuits over keeping prisoners safe during dangerous extreme heat. But U.S.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump shifts approach to Greenland takeover
item.description: What looked like a Trump administration charm offensive in its gambit to take over Greenland, billed as a cultural heritage tour led by Second Lady Usha Vance, has turned into something different with the inclusion of her husband and the explosion of a national security scandal at home.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KTLA 5

item.title: Appeals court refuses to halt an order for the rehiring of thousands of fired federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court in California has refused to halt a judge’s order requiring the Trump administration to rehire thousands of federal workers who were let go in mass firings. A split 9th U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: First rain and then fire chase people from their homes in North and South Carolina
item.description: At least a half-dozen large wildfires continue to burn in the Blue Ridge Mountains of South Carolina and North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins And Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration revokes state and local health funding
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services is canceling tens of billions of dollars in federal grants that state and local health departments were using to track infectious diseases, health disparities, vaccinations, mental health services, and other health issues.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: The MLB teams with the highest
item.description: A California team has the highest median ticket price, a new report found, but they don't play in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: House Democrats demand answers from Trump team about war plans Signal chat
item.description: The top Democrats on the House’s Defense, Oversight, Foreign Affairs, and Intelligence committees want answers from President Trump’s national security team.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Transgender Americans aim to block Trump's passport policy change
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When Ash Lazarus Orr went to renew his passport in early January, the transgender organizer figured it would be relatively routine. But more than two months on, Orr is waiting to get a new passport with a name change and a sex designation reflecting who he is.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs order targeting law firm that employed Mueller team prosecutor
item.description: President Trump signed an order Tuesday targeting a law firm that previously employed Andrew Weissmann, a former federal prosecutor who worked on then-special counsel Robert Mueller’s team.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Tennessee pitcher Karlyn Pickens throws fastest pitch in college softball history
item.description: Tennessee softball standout Karlyn Pickens wrote her name into the history books on Monday with a record-breaking pitch.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, Fox 5

item.title: Consider deleting your 23andMe data now, California AG urges
item.description: The genetic testing company has the genetic data of more than15 million customers — and California Attorney General Rob Bonta has advised users to purge their data.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: Popular rock band's drummer responds after getting fired over 'unfortunate' conduct
item.description: The popular jam band Goose has announced the departure of its drummer over alleged personal "behavior" issues.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Arrests in shooting that killed 3 and hurt 15 at a park in Las Cruces, New Mexico
item.description: LAS CRUCES, N.M. (AP) — A 20-year-old man and three teens have been arrested on suspicion of murder in connection with Friday night's shooting at a park in Las Cruces, New Mexico, that left three people dead and 15 others hurt, authorities said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
